title: VENUS AEROBOT THERMAL CONTROL SUBSYSTEM
org: Team Igneous Seven · NASA L'SPACE Mission Concept Academy
what: Thermal subsystem engineer for the Igneous Seven Venus aerobot, targeting a 370 K equilibrium across a 350–400 K atmospheric profile at 35–50 km altitude with 90 m/s sustained winds

Two-tier passive thermal control architecture combining a low-emissivity chemical coating and an autonomous variable-view-factor radiator — zero power consumption, no sensors or control logic required
how: Convective heat flow analysis quantifying a dominant ~30 kW load (97.8% of total thermal input in the hot case) that shaped the entire system design strategy

Trade studies scoring SilcoNert® 1040 coating (ε = 0.008) and ACT variable-view-factor radiator across reliability, performance, cost, and mass — SilcoNert achieved 100% and ACT scored 84% against competing options
Requirement validation plan using Venus-analog environmental chamber testing, with ±5 K stability around 370 K setpoint as the acceptance criterion
results: 30 kW cooling and 11 kW heating capacity delivered at 0 W power draw — radiator self-regulates via intrinsic vapor-pressure feedback
50× radiative heating reduction via SilcoNert® 1040 coating (466.64 W → 9.33 W), eliminating radiation as a design concern
All thermal requirements verified at Preliminary Design Review; system mass and cost within mission constraints
